chunk_summary: This section focuses on vitamin D repletion, active vitamin D use, and safety considerations for dialysis vitamins in pediatric CKD, emphasizing guidelines for managing mineral bone disorder and micronutrient deficiencies.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of active vitamin D in managing mineral bone disorder in pediatric CKD.
Identify safe practices for vitamin supplementation in children undergoing dialysis.
Review clinical guidelines for vitamin D repletion in pediatric CKD from KDIGO and KDOQI.
Evaluate the risks and benefits of fat-soluble vitamin use in CKD patients.
key_facts: KDIGO 2017 guidelines recommend monitoring serum calcium and phosphate levels during active vitamin D therapy in CKD-MBD management.
Vitamin D repletion is critical for preventing bone deformities and growth retardation in pediatric CKD patients.
Dialysis patients may require tailored vitamin supplementation due to altered metabolism of fat-soluble vitamins.
KDOQI 2008 guidelines emphasize individualized nutritional interventions for children with CKD.
Excessive vitamin D use can lead to hypercalcemia, necessitating careful dosing and monitoring.
reference_quote: KDIGO 2017 guidelines recommend active vitamin D therapy for pediatric CKD patients to manage mineral bone disorder, with careful monitoring of calcium and phosphate levels to prevent complications.
document_key_topics: Vitamin D repletion in pediatric CKD
Active vitamin D use in CKD-MBD management
